title: Satisfying Malty Ethiopian Spinach Azifa
description: A low-calorie, malty Ethiopian dish featuring spinach, this azifa is perfect for a solo dinner, with a bouncy qq texture from the spinach and a savory flavor from the malt and spices.
instructions: Heat a large pan over medium heat and add the chopped onion, cooking until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the chopped spinach to the pan, cooking until it is wilted, about 3-4 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the malt powder, berbere spice, salt, and black pepper.
Add the malt mixture to the pan with the spinach and stir to combine.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the top of the spinach mixture and stir to combine.
Add the water to the pan and bring the mixture to a simmer, cooking for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Remove the pan from the heat and let the azifa cool to room temperature, serving as a solo dinner.
Garnish with additional lemon wedges, if desired, and serve immediately.
